rulings.Case Title: IN RE: AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners Aaa Northern California, Nevada & Utah Insurance'S Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories And Demand For Inspection
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 12, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 8, Petitioners Aaa Northern California, Nevada & Utah Insurance's Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories And Demand For Inspection Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The unopposed motion to compel is granted. Respondents shall serve responses to all outstanding interrogatories and requests for documents by March 27, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highman-ball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SORIBA BANGOURA VS. UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 12, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 4, PLAINTIFF SORIBA BANGOURA'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied. The motion was filed late as to the first and second set of interrogatories since it was filed more than 47 days after the responses were served by express mail. The motion was prematurely filed as to the interrogatories and document requests served on January 25, 2013, since the responses had not yet been served and the time to serve responses had not yet lapsed Also, plaintiff did not attempt to meet and confer with defendants to resolve matters before filing the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highman-ball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 12, 2013, Line 16, PLAINTIFF MARK BIRKHEAD's Motion For Reconsideration. Hearing required. The Court's tentative view is to deny the motion to reconsider pursuant to CCP 1008. The Order filed February 5, 2013 regarding the demurrer states that "Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead, if he can do so in good faith, a cause of action against these defendants." Plaintiff has already filed a First Amended Complaint. If Plaintiff believes there is a basis to make further amendments to his Complaint, he may seek leave of Court to do so if Defendants will not stipulate. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA Y. COLLINS et al VS. NAVISTAR, INC. et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SAN JOAQUIN COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Transferring The Action To The Proper Court; Req. For Snactions Against Law Offices Of Carcione, Cattermole; Etc In The Amount Of $5810.00
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 12, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT NAVISTAR, INC.'s motion For An Order Transferring The Action To The Proper Court; Request For Sanctions Against Law Offices Of Carcione, Cattermole In The Amount Of $5810.00. The motion to transfer pursuant to CCP §396b is denied. Defendant Navistar is not entitled to have this action transferred to San Joaquin County. See Beutke v. American Securities Co (1955) 132 Cal.App.2d 354, 361 ("We are therefore of the opinion that if an action is not commenced in a proper county, as set forth in the Constitution and statutes, the corporation may change the venue to the county of its residence, but there is no authority for changing it to some other county in which the plaintiff could have brought the action.") The Court denies the requests for fees. This order is without prejudice to a motion to transfer the case on other grounds. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE MINZ VS. WALTER WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deft To Appear And Testify At Deposition; And For Order Imposing Sanctions Agaisnt Deft
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 12, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 7, PLAINTIFF JACQUELINE MINZ' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant To Appear And Testify At Deposition; And For Order Imposing Sanctions Agaisnt Defendant Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel is granted. The motion for protective order is also granted. Plaintiff is precluded from asking Ms. Ma questions which assume that she ran over a person unless she clearly admits that she did which so far she hasn't clearly admitted. Sanctions are denied. The parties shall appear at the hearing so that a date is selected for the resumed deposition. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALL CARS TIRE SERVICE, INC. VS. HERNAN CONTRERAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction Enjoining Deft'S Use Of The Dba "Bill Wren'S Towing"
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 12, 2013, Line 24, PLAINTIFF ALL CARS TIRE SERVICE, INC.'s Motion For Preliminary Injunction Enjoining Defendant'S Use Of The Dba "Bill Wren'S Towing". The motion is off calendar because the matter was not timely served. Plaintiff provided 16 court days notice and served the papers by mail and e-mail. The mail service is untimely under CCP 1005. The e-mail service is also untimely under CCP § 1010.6(a)(4) (2 days added to electronic service). If the motion is refiled and served by email, the papers should reflect that there is an agreement between the parties to serve by email. (CCP§ 1010.6(a)(2).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)
